meaning: vt.把...吹胀,激怒,发怒vi.吹气,恫吓,发怒n.发怒,提高价格
examples: She went off in a huff.
She flounced out of my room in a huff.
He was so disappointed that he drove off in a huff.
For about three years (2008 through 2010), Huff is said to have controlled a payroll company.
Sabina in, and after the parish left the conference in a huff after a short period.
